title: [INFORMATION] cannot mount using driver overlay in rootless mode. You need to run it in a `buildah unshare` session
body:
# Description

I am trying to use `buildah` to perform a multi-stage container build similar to the [Docker Multi-Stage Builds](https://docs.docker.com/develop/develop-images/multistage-build/) example. I am able to build it using `podman build` and `buildah bud`. However, when converting it to a shell script using buildah commands, I ran into the following issue:

```bash
++ buildah from docker.io/library/golang:1.16
+ container_base=golang-working-container-4
++ buildah mount golang-working-container-4
cannot mount using driver overlay in rootless mode. You need to run it in a `buildah unshare` session
+ container_base_mount=
```

So, I run `buildah unshare` and it drops me into a shell that then I can run my script and it succeeds. However, I don't know how to integrate that into my script so I don't need to drop into that shell or get the error. I saw Issue https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/2739 and then read the blog: [Tips and Tricks rootless buildah](https://opensource.com/article/19/3/tips-tricks-rootless-buildah). That's where I figured out the second part, but not how to make it work all in one.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
1. Execute Script.

**`buildah_multi-stage_build.sh`**

<details>

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash

function step() {
    # Print a message to stdout with fancy coloring. Usable in ZSH
    #
    # Parameters
    # ----------
    # $1 : string - Message to display to stdout
    local BOLD=$(tput bold)
    local GREEN=$(tput setaf 2)
    local WHITE=$(tput setaf 7)
    local RESET=$(tput sgr0)
    echo
    echo "${BOLD}${GREEN}>>> ${WHITE}$1...${RESET}"
}

set -x
set -e

#step "set unshare"
#buildah unshare

step "Set where we pull our container"
container_from=${1:-docker.io/library/golang:1.16}

step "Pull the image and copy the name"
container_base=$(buildah from ${container_from})
container_base_mount=$(buildah mount ${container_base})

step "Set the working directory"
buildah config --workingdir "/go/src/github.com/alexellis/href-counter/" "${container_base}"

step "go get some libraries"
buildah run "${container_base}" -- go get -d -v golang.org/x/net/html

step "Copy some sources over"
buildah copy "${container_base}" src/golang/app.go ./
buildah copy "${container_base}" src/golang/go.mod ./

step "Build our image"
buildah run "${container_base}" -- /bin/sh -c "CGO_ENABLED=0 GOOS=linux go build -a -installsuffix cgo -o app ."

# Multi-Stage Build Part
step "Get production container"
prod_container_run=$(buildah from docker.io/library/alpine:latest)
step "mount the container"
prod_container_mount=$(buildah mount ${container_base})

step "install ca-certificates"
buildah run "${prod_container_run}" -- apk --no-cache add ca-certificates

step "set working directory"
buildah config --workingdir "/root/" "${prod_container_run}"

step "copy the previous artifact to our prod container"
buildah copy "${prod_container_run}" ${container_base_mount}/go/src/github.com/alexellis/href-counter//app .

step "Set the container command"
buildah config --cmd ./app "${prod_container_run}"

#step "unmount the builder"
#buildah unmount ${container_base_mount}
#step "unmount the prod mount"
#buildah unmount ${prod_container_mount}

step "Commit the container"
buildah commit --rm "${prod_container_run}" multi-stage:latest
```

</details>

**`app.go`**

<details>

```go
package main

import "fmt"

func main() {
	fmt.Println("Hello, World!")
}
```

</details>

**`go.mod`**

<details>

```go
module github.com/filbot3/golang_hello_world

go 1.16
```

</details>


**Describe the results you received:**

<details>

```bash
➜  buildah_examples git:(main) ✗ ./src/bash/buildah_multi-stage_build.sh
+ set -e
+ step 'Set where we pull our container'

+ echo

>>> Pull the image and copy the name...

+ container_base=golang-working-container-4
++ buildah mount golang-working-container-4
cannot mount using driver overlay in rootless mode. You need to run it in a `buildah unshare` session
+ container_base_mount=
```

comments.body: @FilBot3 what happens when you do `buildah unshare script.sh`? , you don't need to open the shell interactively you can just do a `buildah unshare script.sh`
you cannot use `mount` at all in rootless mode without `unshare`.

Running the `buildah unshare script.sh` did work as expected and not drop me into another shell. Thank you @flouthoc !

Now this makes more sense

```bash
➜  ~ buildah unshare -h

  Runs a command in a modified user namespace.

Usage:
  buildah unshare [flags]

Examples:
  buildah unshare id
  buildah unshare cat /proc/self/uid_map
  buildah unshare buildah-script.sh

Flags:
  -h, --help            help for unshare
      --mount strings   mount the specified containers (default [])
```
